output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Get It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and send a legal demand letter in 60 seconds for only $19.99. No signup needed.
content_outline: Why landlords illegally withhold security deposits
State laws: deadlines and what landlords can actually deduct
How to write a security deposit demand letter that gets results
Escalation tactics: penalties, small claims court, and tenant rights
key_points: Most U.S. states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days
Normal wear and tear cannot be legally deducted from a security deposit
A formal demand letter citing state statute is the fastest way to recover funds without hiring a lawyer
output.cta: Stop waiting. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds—just $19.99, no signup or subscription required.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters whose landlords are illegally withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and need fast, affordable legal leverage.

They legally cannot keep your deposit for normal wear and tear. Here’s the script that got mine back in 48 hours. 📝
Stop asking 'where’s my deposit?' Start sending this instead. 💪

short_form_scripts.hook: Your landlord has 21 days to return your deposit. If they don’t, they’re breaking the law.
short_form_scripts.body: Most tenants don’t know that state laws strictly limit how long a landlord can hold your money. If they miss the deadline or invent fake repair fees, you can often demand double—or even triple—your deposit back. But first, you need the right letter.
short_form_scripts.cta: Go to Deposits-Back.com. Answer a few questions and get a state-specific demand letter in 60 seconds. Just $19.99, no signup required.

short_form_scripts.hook: My landlord said 'the deposit is non-refundable'... that’s illegal. 🚩
short_form_scripts.body: Security deposits are ALWAYS refundable unless you caused actual damage beyond normal wear and tear. Carpet fade, nail holes, and scuffs? Not damage. If they’re withholding your money, you don’t need a lawyer yet—you need a demand letter that cites your exact state statute.
short_form_scripts.cta: Generate yours now at Deposits-Back.com for $19.99. 60 seconds and you’re done.

short_form_scripts.hook: What to do when your landlord doesn’t return your deposit 👇
short_form_scripts.body: Step 1: Document everything. Step 2: Send a formal demand letter with your state’s legal deadline and deduction rules. Step 3: If they still ignore you, take them to small claims court—where judges frequently award 2-3x the deposit. The letter is what makes Step 3 a slam dunk.
short_form_scripts.cta: Write your demand letter in 60 seconds at Deposits-Back.com. No subscription, no hassle—just $19.99.
